# ADR-0007: distribute the operator skill as a self-installing plugin package

> **Status:** accepted | **Date:** 2026-07-04

**References:** [RFC-0008:C-AGENT-PLUGIN](../rfc/RFC-0008.md#rfc-0008c-agent-plugin), [RFC-0004:C-INFERLAB-TOOLCHAIN](../rfc/RFC-0004.md#rfc-0004c-inferlab-toolchain)

## Context

Inferlab is being published to GitHub, and agents (Claude Code, Codex) are first-class operators of the CLI. Shipping an operator skill as a bare skills/ directory with manual copy installation offers no lifecycle: no update, no uninstall, no diagnosis, and copies drift silently from the shipped content. A maintained distribution pattern exists: the repository root doubles as a plugin marketplace (.claude-plugin/marketplace.json for Claude, .agents/plugins/marketplace.json for Codex), the plugin package lives under plugins/<name>/ with per-runtime manifests and the skills, and the product binary embeds the crates.io agent-plugin-installer library to drive the native `claude plugin` / `codex plugin` CLIs for install, update, uninstall, and doctor. The skill content itself is written against the current CLI surface.

## Decision

Ship the operator skill as a repo-root plugin package and add an `inferlab agent install|update|uninstall|doctor` command group embedding the agent-plugin-installer library (pinned in the workspace Cargo.toml; the lock selects the exact version). Concrete layout in this repository: `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (Claude marketplace), `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` (Codex marketplace), and the package under `plugins/inferlab/` with per-runtime plugin manifests and `skills/inferlab/SKILL.md`. Install operates on a local checkout or unpacked release tarball supplied explicitly by path. Reasons over the alternatives: (1) one code path gives both runtimes the full lifecycle instead of copy-time-only installation; (2) validate-before-invoke turns a broken package into a precise error; (3) the repo doubling as the marketplace keeps installation offline-capable. The normative obligations of the command group live in [RFC-0008:C-AGENT-PLUGIN](../rfc/RFC-0008.md#rfc-0008c-agent-plugin); this ADR records why that surface exists and why this shape won.

## Consequences

Positive:
- One command installs, updates, or removes the skill on both runtimes, with doctor diagnosing missing native CLIs; no manual file copying.
- The package is validated before the native CLI runs, so a broken checkout fails loudly with the missing path named.
- The repo doubles as the marketplace: a git checkout or the release skills tarball is sufficient; no external marketplace dependency.

Negative:
- The plugin lifecycle depends on the native CLIs' plugin subcommands remaining stable; a breaking change there surfaces as loud agent-command failures until the pinned library is bumped.
- The skill text can drift from the CLI surface as it evolves — the standing failure mode of shipped operator documentation. No mechanical guard is built (per the [ADR-0006](../adr/ADR-0006.md) bar); release-time review of the skill against the command surface owns this explicitly.
- One more public command group to document and keep small.

Neutral:
- The skill content is versioned with the repo and pinned into release artifacts; updating an installed skill is an explicit `inferlab agent update`.

## Alternatives Considered

### Embedded installer command over a repo-root marketplace package: plugins/inferlab with per-runtime manifests, .claude-plugin and .agents/plugins marketplace files, and an inferlab agent command group embedding agent-plugin-installer (accepted)

- **Pros:** full lifecycle (install/update/uninstall/doctor) on both runtimes through one code path, package validated before the native CLI runs; offline-capable from a checkout or release tarball
- **Cons:** depends on native plugin CLI stability and one crates.io library

### Raw-copy installation: install.sh copies SKILL.md into each agent's skills directory (rejected)

- **Pros:** no new command or dependency
- **Cons:** no update or uninstall lifecycle; copies drift silently from plugin-managed state; per-runtime layouts hand-maintained

### Central marketplace distribution only: publish the plugin to an external marketplace and point users there (rejected)

- **Pros:** discoverable without cloning the repo
- **Cons:** adds an external distribution dependency; a checkout or tarball must stay installable without network anyway
